title: Alpha Futures Pricing Explained: All Plans, Costs & Fees (2026)

excerpt: Complete breakdown of Alpha Futures pricing — all plans, hidden fees, and how to save money.
content:
Alpha Futures Pricing Overview

Alpha Futures is a UK-based prop trading firm founded in 2024 that offers funded trading accounts with a transparent, tiered pricing model. Starting at $79 per month, Alpha Futures provides three distinct account types—Standard, Advanced, and Zero—each designed for different trader profiles and capital levels. With a 4.7/5 rating and exceptional 4.9/5 Trustpilot score across 2,500+ reviews, the firm has quickly established itself as a competitive player in the futures prop trading space. Unlike some competitors, Alpha Futures keeps its fee structure straightforward: monthly subscription pricing with no hidden evaluation fees, and payouts processed within 48 business hours across multiple rails (ACH, Wire, SWIFT, Wise, and Rise).

All Alpha Futures Plans Compared

Alpha Futures structures its offering around three core product lines, each with two capital tiers. Understanding which plan fits your trading style requires looking at the specific features, profit splits, and drawdown rules baked into each.

Plan Capital Monthly Cost Profit Split Key Feature
Standard $50K $79/mo 80% (first 5 payouts), 90% after EOD trailing drawdown
Standard $100K $159/mo 80% (first 5 payouts), 90% after EOD trailing drawdown
Advanced $50K $139/mo 90% from day one No consistency rule, immediate 90%
Advanced $100K $279/mo 90% from day one No consistency rule, immediate 90%
Zero $25K $79/mo 80% (first 5 payouts), 90% after Entry-level capital, same cost as Standard $50K
Zero $50K $119/mo 80% (first 5 payots), 90% after Mid-range evaluation stepping stone

Standard Plans ($79–$159/mo)

Standard accounts use EOD (end-of-day) trailing drawdown, meaning your maximum loss is calculated at market close each day. This is crucial for day traders: you cannot trigger a drawdown on an intraday wick. You start at 80% profit split for your first five payouts, then graduate to 90%. Weekly payouts begin immediately upon five consecutive winning days, keeping money in motion. The $50K tier costs $79/month, making it the most affordable entry point for traders seeking genuine EOD risk management.

Advanced Plans ($139–$279/mo)

Advanced plans command a premium ($60–$120 higher monthly cost) because they unlock 90% profit split from day one with no consistency or payout requirement attached. There is no 5-payout waiting period; funded traders begin earning the firm's maximum share immediately. This appeals to experienced traders with proven track records who don't need the evaluation phase and want to maximize earnings instantly.

Zero Plans ($79–$119/mo)

Zero accounts are Alpha Futures' entry-level offering, starting with just $25K capital. At $79/month, the $25K tier matches Standard plan pricing despite 50% less capital, effectively doubling your monthly cost per dollar of account size. However, it provides a low-stakes way to prove your edge before scaling to $50K or $100K. The $50K Zero plan at $119/month falls between Standard $50K ($79) and Advanced $50K ($139), positioning it as a logical stepping stone for traders graduating from evaluation.

Free Plan / Free Trial

Alpha Futures does not offer a free tier or free trial. You must pay the monthly subscription fee to access a funded account. However, the firm does provide paper trading functionality at no additional cost once you're subscribed, allowing you to test strategies and platform mechanics before committing real capital to live trading. This is standard practice across most prop firms and helps traders familiarize themselves with execution without risk.

Hidden Costs and Fees

Alpha Futures maintains a relatively transparent fee structure, but several costs deserve attention:

  • Monthly Subscription Only. Unlike some competitors, Alpha Futures charges a flat monthly fee with no separate evaluation fee, performance surcharge, or commission-per-trade model.
  • Exchange and Data Fees. The pricing provided does not explicitly mention exchange fees or real-time data costs, which may be bundled or passed through depending on your broker integration.
  • Platform Lock-In. A notable operational constraint: your platform choice is locked in at purchase and cannot be switched mid-account. If you select a broker but later want to switch, you may need to close and open a new account, incurring another month of fees.
  • News Buffer Enforcement. Standard and Zero qualified accounts enforce a 2-minute news buffer around major economic announcements. This is a risk-control feature, not a direct cost, but it reduces trading opportunity during volatile windows.
  • Geographic Payout Considerations. While Alpha Futures offers multiple payout rails (ACH, Wire, SWIFT, Wise, Rise), the firm's UK domicile may add friction for US-based traders on certain withdrawal methods, though 48-hour processing is competitive.

Alpha Futures Pricing vs Competitors

Alpha Futures positions itself in the mid-range of the prop trading market. Typical competitors charge $50–$200/month for similar $25K–$100K accounts, making Alpha Futures competitively priced. The distinguishing factors are:

  • Profit Split Speed. The Advanced plan's immediate 90% payout is faster than many competitors' phased approaches. Traders on Standard plans reach 90% after just 5 winning days, a short evaluation window compared to firms requiring consistency over weeks.
  • EOD Trailing Drawdown. The EOD rule (no intraday liquidation on wicks) is a feature not all prop firms highlight or offer, giving day traders a structural advantage.
  • Payout Rails. Offering ACH, Wire, SWIFT, Wise, and Rise is broader than many single-rail competitors, and 48-hour processing is inline with industry standards.
  • No Algo/Bot Trading. Expert Advisors, algorithmic bots, and high-frequency trading are prohibited across all accounts. If you rely on automation, Alpha Futures is not a fit regardless of price.

Is Alpha Futures Worth the Price?

Standard Plans: Best For Day Traders on a Budget

At $79/month for $50K capital, Standard accounts offer exceptional value for discretionary day traders. The EOD trailing drawdown is the standout feature—it eliminates the risk of being liquidated on an intraday wick, which is a material concern in high-volatility futures. The 80%-to-90% progression is standard market practice and motivates consistency. Payouts are weekly, keeping capital liquid. If you trade intraday and have $50K to start, this is hard to beat.

Advanced Plans: For Serious Traders Ready to Scale

Advanced plans ($139–$279/mo) justify their premium for traders with a proven edge who want zero evaluation friction. Paying 90% from day one rather than 80% compounds significantly if you're generating consistent profits. At a $100K scale, the extra $120/month is trivial compared to the value of immediate 90% payouts. This tier is best for traders who've already proven themselves elsewhere and want the fastest path to maximum earnings.

Zero Plans: Entry Point, Not a Long-Term Hold

Zero accounts ($25K at $79/mo) are a gateway. The monthly cost-to-capital ratio is unfavorable compared to Standard, but useful for traders testing Alpha Futures before committing larger capital. The $50K Zero tier ($119/mo) is the logical next step, splitting the difference between Standard and Advanced pricing.

Upgrade Triggers

Consider upgrading when you're consistently profitable on your current tier and want faster payouts or higher capital. Moving from Standard to Advanced makes sense if your monthly profits exceed $200–$300, where the 10% split difference justifies the premium. Scaling from $50K to $100K should be driven by trading volume and profitability, not by the price—the monthly cost increase is minimal relative to the opportunity.

How to Save on Alpha Futures

Alpha Futures' pricing is straightforward with limited discount options, but several strategies can reduce your effective cost:

  • Start at the Zero $25K Tier. Test the platform and rules at the lowest monthly cost ($79) before committing to larger capital tiers. If you're profitable within 30 days, scaling to $50K or $100K is a data-driven decision.
  • Maximize Payout Frequency. Both Standard and Advanced accounts support weekly payouts after 5 consecutive winning days. By maintaining consistency, you reduce the need to reinvest profits into the account, effectively lowering your carry cost.
  • Leverage Paper Trading. Use included paper trading to perfect your strategy before going live. This eliminates the cost of learning on a funded account.
  • Platform Selection Matters. Since platform choice locks in at purchase, choose a broker integration you'll stay with long-term to avoid paying a second month of fees on a new account.
  • Annual Billing (If Available). Check if Alpha Futures offers annual prepay discounts, common in the SaaS/prop space. A 10–15% annual discount would save $95–$180 per year on a $79/month account.

Bottom Line: Alpha Futures prices competitively with transparent terms, fast payouts, and risk rules favorable to day traders. The real value is in the EOD trailing drawdown and profit split structure—not in chasing discounts. Choose the tier that matches your capital and edge, and optimize from there.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
